channel.title: Tesla Shorts Time Daily
channel.description: A daily podcast covering the latest Tesla news, stock prices, and industry insights. AI Disclosure: This podcast is curated by Patrick but uses AI-generated voice synthesis (ElevenLabs) for audio production.

item.title: Ep 408: Elon Musk confirms Tesla's Terafab AI chip plant starts production in just seven days.
item.description:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** March 15, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $391.20 ▼ $4.28 (1.1%)
**HOOK:** Elon Musk confirms Tesla's Terafab AI chip plant starts production in just seven days.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Why Is The Tesla Semi Still In Pilot Program Stage? — 14 March, 2026, 7:42 PM PST, CleanTechnica** Tesla's Semi truck, unveiled almost a decade ago, remains in pilot stages with companies like Mone Transport now joining trials, highlighting slower-than-expected scaling. This matters for the trucking industry as electric semis could cut emissions drastically, but delays point to challenges in battery tech and infrastructure. For Tesla, it's about proving real-world viability to accelerate adoption and dominate heavy-duty EV market share. Source: https://cleantechnica.com/2026/03/14/why-is-the-tesla-semi-still-in-pilot-program-stage/
2. **Tesla’s Terafab Project will Launch Next Week — 14 March, 2026, 4:00 PM PST, Drive Tesla** Elon Musk announced Tesla's Terafab, an AI chip manufacturing initiative, kicks off in seven days, building on plans shared at last year's shareholder meeting. This is huge for Tesla's tech edge, as in-house chips could power better autonomous driving and robots, reducing reliance on external suppliers. It signals a strategic shift toward vertical integration, potentially boosting efficiency and cutting costs in the competitive AI space. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/teslas-terafab-project-will-launch-next-week/
3. **Tesla Terafab Alert: Elon Musk Confirms AI Chip Plant Starts in 7 Days — 14 March, 2026, 9:29 AM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Elon Musk just verified that Tesla's Terafab project, focused on domestic AI chip production, launches in a week amid North Campus expansions. This development is key for powering Tesla's autonomous fleet and Optimus robots, addressing supply chain vulnerabilities in the AI boom. It positions Tesla as a leader in semiconductor self-sufficiency, which could reshape the auto industry's tech landscape. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/14/tesla-terafab-alert-elon-musk-confirms-ai-chip-plant-starts-in-7-days/
4. **Ontario Keeps Cost of Cancelled Starlink Contract Secret — 14 March, 2026, 7:23 AM PST, Drive Tesla** The Ontario government is withholding details on the cancellation fee for a scrapped Starlink deal, with Premier Doug Ford noting it was far less than the full $100 million value. While not directly Tesla, this ties into Elon's ecosystem and highlights political hurdles for satellite internet expansion in Canada. It underscores regulatory risks for tech deployments, potentially affecting broader adoption of connected services in remote areas. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/ontario-keeps-cost-of-cancelled-starlink-contract-secret/
5. **What is Digital Optimus? The new Tesla and xAI project explained — 14 March, 2026, 5:59 AM PST, Teslarati** Digital Optimus, a collaboration between Tesla and xAI, uses a dual-process architecture mimicking human cognition to advance AI capabilities. This could revolutionize Tesla's robotics and autonomous systems by enabling more intuitive decision-making. For the industry, it means pushing boundaries in AI ethics and efficiency, giving Tesla a thoughtful edge in humanoid tech development. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/tesla-xai-digital-optimus-explained/
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ## Tesla X Takeover: What's Hot Right Now 🎙️ Tesla X Takeover - What's breaking in the Tesla world today! Here are the most interesting, fresh Tesla developments that have everyone talking.
1. **Tesla Rolls Out FSD Version 14** - Tesla has released the latest Full Self-Driving update, promising smoother navigation and better handling of complex urban scenarios. This is exciting because it addresses user feedback on edge cases, potentially accelerating regulatory approval for wider rollout. Community buzz is high, with owners sharing early vi...
AI Disclosure: This podcast is curated by Patrick but uses AI-generated voice synthesis (ElevenLabs) for audio production.

item.title: Ep 406: Tesla gains a stake in SpaceX by converting its $2B xAI investment, deepening ties between Musk's companies.
item.description:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** March 13, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $395.01 ▼ $0.47 (0.1%)
**HOOK:** Tesla gains a stake in SpaceX by converting its $2B xAI investment, deepening ties between Musk's companies.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Lucid unveils Lunar Robotaxi in bid to challenge Tesla’s Cybercab in the autonomous ride hailing race: 13 March, 2026, 12:28 AM PDT, Teslarati** Lucid has introduced its Lunar robotaxi concept, directly aiming to compete with Tesla's Cybercab in the growing autonomous ride-hailing market. This move highlights increasing competition in self-driving tech, potentially pushing Tesla to accelerate Cybercab deployments and innovations to maintain its lead in robotaxi services. For the industry, it signals a heating up of the race for affordable, scalable autonomous vehicles that could transform urban mobility. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/lucid-lunar-robotaxi-concept-vs-tesla-cybercab-ridehailing/
2. **Tesla Model Y RWD now eligible for Canada’s $5,000 EVAP rebate: 12 March, 2026, 8:14 PM PDT, Drive Tesla** Tesla's Model Y Rear-Wheel Drive now qualifies for Canada's federal $5,000 EV affordability rebate, making it more accessible for buyers. This eligibility could boost sales in Canada by lowering the effective price, helping Tesla expand its market share in a region with strong EV incentives. For customers, it means greater affordability for a popular SUV, potentially accelerating EV adoption amid rising fuel costs. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/model-y/tesla-model-y-rwd-now-eligible-for-canadas-5000-evap-rebate/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=tesla-model-y-rwd-now-eligible-for-canadas-5000-evap-rebate
3. **Tesla Launches Three-Row Model Y L in Australia and New Zealand, Deliveries Start in Q2 2026: 12 March, 2026, 5:44 PM PDT, Drive Tesla** Tesla has started taking orders for the six-seat Model Y L in Australia and New Zealand, with deliveries slated for Q2 2026 at starting prices of $74,900 AUD and $83,900 NZD. This expansion addresses demand for family-sized EVs in these markets, strengthening Tesla's lineup against local competitors. It matters for Tesla's global growth, as it taps into regions with high EV interest but limited options for larger vehicles. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/model-y/tesla-launches-three-row-model-y-l-in-australia-and-new-zealand-deliveries-start-in-q2-2026/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=tesla-launches-three-row-model-y-l-in-australia-and-new-zealand-deliveries-start-in-q2-2026
4. **Tesla Gains SpaceX Ownership After Converting $2B xAI Investment: 12 March, 2026, 3:34 PM PDT, Drive Tesla** Tesla has converted its $2 billion investment in xAI into a minority stake in SpaceX following FTC approval. This financial maneuver ties Tesla closer to SpaceX's innovations, potentially benefiting areas like satellite tech for vehicle connectivity. For Tesla's business, it diversifies assets ahead of SpaceX's possible IPO, enhancing long-term value for shareholders. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/tesla-gains-spacex-ownership-after-converting-2b-xai-investment/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=tesla-gains-spacex-ownership-after-converting-2b-xai-investment
5. **Tesla China Sales Surge 91% in February Despite Broader Market Slump: 12 March, 2026, 2:21 PM PDT, TeslaNorth.com** Tesla delivered 58,599 vehicles from Gigafactory Shanghai in February, marking a 91% year-over-year increase amid a declining Chinese auto market. This surge underscores Tesla's resilience through competitive pricing and strong export strategies. It positions Tesla well in the world's largest EV market, potentially offsetting slumps elsewhere by bolstering overall production numbers. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/12/tesla-china-sales-surge-91-in-february-despite-broader-market-slump/
6. **Tesla Launches New 4-in-1 USB-C Hub for Model 3 and Model Y: 12 March, 2026, 12:33 PM PDT, TeslaNor...

item.title: Ep 404: Tesla ramps up Cybercab testing on public roads as it queues for mass production.
item.description:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** March 11, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $399.24 ▼ $0.55 (0.1%)
**HOOK:** Tesla ramps up Cybercab testing on public roads as it queues for mass production.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Tesla Cybercab display highlights interior wizardry in the small two-seater: 11 March, 2026, 01:33 AM PST, Teslarati** Photos and videos of the production Cybercab's interior have surfaced on social media, showcasing advanced features in the compact two-seater design. This highlights Tesla's focus on efficient, minimalist cabins for autonomous vehicles, which could lower costs and improve user experience in urban mobility. For the industry, it signals accelerating progress toward affordable robotaxis, potentially disrupting ride-sharing services. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/tesla-cybercab-display-interior-wizardry-small-two-seater/
2. **Tesla China teases Optimus robot’s human-looking next-gen hands: 11 March, 2026, 12:50 AM PST, Teslarati** Tesla shared an image of Optimus robot's upgraded hands on Weibo, emphasizing more human-like dexterity for complex tasks. This advancement matters for Tesla's robotics ambitions, enabling better integration into manufacturing and daily assistance, which could boost productivity. It positions Tesla as a leader in humanoid robotics, influencing broader AI and automation sectors. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/tesla-optimus-human-like-hands-teaser/
3. **Tesla Semi expands pilot program to Texas logistics firm: here’s what they said: 10 March, 2026, 09:29 AM PST, Teslarati** A Texas logistics company tested the Tesla Semi, reporting 1.64 kWh per mile efficiency, surpassing Tesla's targets and outperforming diesel trucks. This expands Tesla's real-world data on heavy-duty EVs, strengthening its case for sustainable freight transport and reducing operational costs for fleets. It could accelerate adoption in logistics, impacting emissions and fuel economics in the trucking industry. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/tesla-semi-expands-pilot-program-texas-based-logistics-firm-what-they-said/
4. **Tesla Sues Former Supplier for $1 Billion Over Alleged Battery Tech Theft: 10 March, 2026, 02:18 PM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Tesla filed a lawsuit against Matthews International, accusing it of stealing trade secrets on dry electrode battery manufacturing and sharing them with competitors. This legal move protects Tesla's proprietary tech, crucial for maintaining its edge in battery efficiency and cost reduction. For the EV industry, it underscores the high stakes of intellectual property in advancing sustainable energy solutions. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/10/tesla-sues-former-supplier-for-1-billion-over-alleged-battery-tech-theft/
5. **Tesla Cybercab Camera Washers Caught in Action at Giga Texas: 10 March, 2026, 09:48 AM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Footage from Giga Texas shows the Cybercab's automated camera washers using high-pressure fluid to maintain clear vision in bad weather. This feature is vital for reliable autonomous operation, addressing a key challenge in sensor-dependent vehicles. It demonstrates Tesla's engineering focus on all-weather performance, potentially setting new standards for robotaxi safety and uptime. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/10/tesla-cybercab-camera-washers-caught-in-action-at-giga-texas/
6. **Tesla Model Y Performance deliveries in Canada expected soon, but not for everyone: 10 March, 2026, 07:30 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Tesla plans to start delivering Model Y Performance vehicles in Canada soon, beginning in Ontario, though not all customers will get them immediately. This rollout matters for Tesla's market expansion in North America, helping meet demand for high-performance EVs amid supply constraints. It could influence customer satisfaction and competitive positioning against other EV makers in the region. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/tesla...

item.title: Ep 402: Tesla's FSD tech is now called more revolutionary than the iPhone by experts, sparking major buzz.
item.description:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** March 09, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $396.73 ▲ $2.04 (0.5%)
**HOOK:** Tesla's FSD tech is now called more revolutionary than the iPhone by experts, sparking major buzz.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **The Boring Company’s Vegas Loop moves 82k riders during CONEXPO: 09 March, 2026, 2:10 AM PST, Teslarati** The Boring Company's Vegas Loop transported over 82,000 riders during the CONEXPO event, showcasing its growing capacity for high-volume urban transport. This milestone highlights how underground tunneling can alleviate traffic congestion in busy areas like Las Vegas, potentially opening new revenue streams for related ventures. For Tesla, it underscores the synergies with Elon's ecosystem, though it's more about broader mobility innovation than direct EV business. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/boring-company-vegas-loop-82000-riders-conexpo/
2. **Tesla FSD is More Revolutionary Than the iPhone, Experts Now Claim: 08 March, 2026, 8:54 AM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Experts and users, including podcaster Clay Travis, are praising Tesla's Full Self-Driving software as a game-changer surpassing the iPhone's impact, describing it as otherworldly. This shift in perception could boost consumer adoption and investor confidence in Tesla's autonomous tech roadmap. It matters for the industry as it positions Tesla as a leader in AI-driven mobility, potentially pressuring competitors to accelerate their own self-driving efforts. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/08/tesla-fsd-is-more-revolutionary-than-the-iphone-experts-now-claim/
3. **Tesla Opens First Los Angeles Megacharger for Semi Customers: 08 March, 2026, 8:42 AM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Tesla launched its first Megacharger station in the Los Angeles area at Ontario, California, aimed at supporting Semi truck fleets with heavy-duty charging. This expansion targets logistics hubs like the Inland Empire, facilitating faster adoption of electric trucks in freight transport. For Tesla's business, it strengthens the Semi program's viability, helping to decarbonize commercial shipping while creating new infrastructure revenue opportunities. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/08/tesla-opens-first-los-angeles-megacharger-for-semi-customers/
4. **Tesla App Leak: New V2G Feature Lets Your Car Earn You Money: 08 March, 2026, 8:37 AM PST, TeslaNorth.com** A leak in Tesla's app code reveals upcoming Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) features that allow EVs to sell power back to the grid during peak times, potentially earning owners money through Tesla Electric. This could transform vehicles into mobile energy assets, enhancing grid stability and owner incentives. It means a lot for customers, offering practical financial benefits, while advancing Tesla's energy business in a competitive renewable market. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/08/tesla-app-leak-new-v2g-feature-lets-your-car-earn-you-money/
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ## Tesla X Takeover: What's Hot Right Now 🎙️ Tesla X Takeover - What's breaking in the Tesla world today! Here are the most interesting, fresh Tesla developments that have everyone talking.
1. **Cybertruck Production Hits New Milestone** - Tesla announced ramping up Cybertruck output to 1,000 units per week at Giga Texas. This surge is exciting because it shows Tesla overcoming early manufacturing hurdles, potentially flooding the market with more affordable electric pickups. It's generating buzz in the community as owners share customization stories, highlighting how it's reshaping the truck segment.
2. **Robotaxi Service Trials Begin in Austin** - Early tests of Tesla's Robotaxi fleet started in Austin, with select users getting autonomous rides. What's noteworthy is the real-world data collection that's accelerating FSD improvements, though some hiccups like navigation errors are sparking honest discussions. This could redefine urban transport if scaled, making Tesla a key player in ride-sharing evolut...

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **The Boring Company’s Vegas Loop moves 82k riders during CONEXPO: 09 March, 2026, 2:10 AM PST, Teslarati** The Boring Company's Vegas Loop transported over 82,000 riders during the CONEXPO event, showcasing its growing capacity for high-volume urban transport. This milestone highlights how underground tunneling can alleviate traffic congestion in busy areas like Las Vegas, potentially opening new revenue streams for related ventures. For Tesla, it underscores the synergies with Elon's ecosystem, though it's more about broader mobility innovation than direct EV business. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/boring-company-vegas-loop-82000-riders-conexpo/
2. **Tesla FSD is More Revolutionary Than the iPhone, Experts Now Claim: 08 March, 2026, 8:54 AM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Experts and users, including podcaster Clay Travis, are praising Tesla's Full Self-Driving software as a game-changer surpassing the iPhone's impact, describing it as otherworldly. This shift in perception could boost consumer adoption and investor confidence in Tesla's autonomous tech roadmap. It matters for the industry as it positions Tesla as a leader in AI-driven mobility, potentially pressuring competitors to accelerate their own self-driving efforts. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/08/tesla-fsd-is-more-revolutionary-than-the-iphone-experts-now-claim/
3. **Tesla Opens First Los Angeles Megacharger for Semi Customers: 08 March, 2026, 8:42 AM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Tesla launched its first Megacharger station in the Los Angeles area at Ontario, California, aimed at supporting Semi truck fleets with heavy-duty charging. This expansion targets logistics hubs like the Inland Empire, facilitating faster adoption of electric trucks in freight transport. For Tesla's business, it strengthens the Semi program's viability, helping to decarbonize commercial shipping while creating new infrastructure revenue opportunities. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/08/tesla-opens-first-los-angeles-megacharger-for-semi-customers/
4. **Tesla App Leak: New V2G Feature Lets Your Car Earn You Money: 08 March, 2026, 8:37 AM PST, TeslaNorth.com** A leak in Tesla's app code reveals upcoming Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) features that allow EVs to sell power back to the grid during peak times, potentially earning owners money through Tesla Electric. This could transform vehicles into mobile energy assets, enhancing grid stability and owner incentives. It means a lot for customers, offering practical financial benefits, while advancing Tesla's energy business in a competitive renewable market. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/08/tesla-app-leak-new-v2g-feature-lets-your-car-earn-you-money/
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ## Tesla X Takeover: What's Hot Right Now 🎙️ Tesla X Takeover - What's breaking in the Tesla world today! Here are the most interesting, fresh Tesla developments that have everyone talking.
1. **Cybertruck Production Hits New Milestone** - Tesla announced ramping up Cybertruck output to 1,000 units per week at Giga Texas. This surge is exciting because it shows Tesla overcoming early manufacturing hurdles, potentially flooding the market with more affordable electric pickups. It's generating buzz in the community as owners share customization stories, highlighting how it's reshaping the truck segment.
2. **Robotaxi Service Trials Begin in Austin** - Early tests of Tesla's Robotaxi fleet started in Austin, with select users getting autonomous rides. What's noteworthy is the real-world data collection that's accelerating FSD improvements, though some hiccups like navigation errors are sparking honest discussions. This could redefine urban transport if scaled, making Tesla a key player in ride-sharing evolut...

item.title: Ep 400: Tesla's patent for a one-piece composite seat signals the next-gen Roadster is nearing its debut.
item.description:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** March 06, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $405.55 ▲ $0.07 (0.0%)
**HOOK:** Tesla's patent for a one-piece composite seat signals the next-gen Roadster is nearing its debut.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Tesla Roadster patent hints at radical seat redesign ahead of reveal: 05 March, 2026, 11:17 PM PST, Teslarati** Tesla published a patent for a "vehicle seat system" using a single continuous composite frame, moving away from traditional multi-part seats to reduce weight and complexity. This innovation could enhance performance in high-speed vehicles like the Roadster, potentially improving acceleration and handling while cutting manufacturing costs. For Tesla's business, it underscores ongoing R&D investment in lightweight materials, which might give an edge in the competitive sports EV market. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/tesla-roadster-seat-patent-feature-highlights/
2. **Tesla opens two new Superchargers in Laval and Quebec City [Quebec]: 05 March, 2026, 02:31 PM PST, Drive Tesla** Tesla added Supercharger stations on Avenue du Cosmodôme in Laval and Boulevard de l’Auvergne in Quebec City, expanding its charging network in the province. This matters for customers as it improves accessibility for long-distance travel in colder climates, potentially boosting EV adoption in Canada. From an industry perspective, it reinforces Tesla's lead in infrastructure, pressuring competitors to match the pace. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/tesla-opens-two-new-superchargers-in-laval-and-quebec-city-quebec/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=tesla-opens-two-new-superchargers-in-laval-and-quebec-city-quebec
3. **Tesla Begins FSD Testing in Tokyo Ahead of 2026 Japan Launch: 05 March, 2026, 01:57 PM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Tesla started public demos of Full Self-Driving Supervised in Tokyo using Model 3 and Model Y vehicles to test performance in dense urban settings. This step is crucial for adapting the software to Japan's unique traffic and regulations, aiming for a late 2026 launch. It highlights Tesla's push into new markets, which could drive revenue growth but requires navigating local safety standards. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/05/tesla-begins-fsd-testing-in-tokyo-ahead-of-2026-japan-launch/
4. **Tesla Offers First Glimpse Inside Massive Semi Factory in Nevada: 05 March, 2026, 01:52 PM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Journalist Ashlee Vance shared images of Tesla's expansive Semi factory in Nevada, showcasing its scale and operations for the electric Class 8 truck. This visibility matters for Tesla's manufacturing ambitions, demonstrating progress in scaling production amid delays. For the industry, it signals Tesla's intent to disrupt trucking with sustainable options, potentially attracting more fleet orders. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/05/tesla-offers-first-glimpse-inside-massive-semi-factory-in-nevada/
5. **Tesla wins another award critics will absolutely despise: 05 March, 2026, 12:11 PM PST, Teslarati** Tesla scored 49% overall in a sustainability ranking, leading over Ford with strong marks in emissions reduction and decarbonization. This recognition validates Tesla's environmental efforts despite skepticism, which could enhance its brand appeal to eco-conscious investors and customers. In the broader industry, it pressures legacy automakers to accelerate their green transitions. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/tesla-wins-another-award-critics-absolutely-despise/
6. **Tesla issues fix for Model Y front camera water ingress in new Service Bulletin: 05 March, 2026, 08:35 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Tesla released a service bulletin addressing water intrusion in the Model Y's front bumper camera, providing a fix to prevent failures. This is important for vehicle reliability, especially in wet climates, ensuring safety features like Autopilot remain functional. It reflects Tesla's proactive approach to quality control, which helps m...

item.title: Ep 398: Automakers are exiting Tesla's EU carbon credit pool, which could squeeze a key revenue stream worth billions.
item.description:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** March 04, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $405.94 ▲ $15.34 (3.9%)
**HOOK:** Automakers are exiting Tesla's EU carbon credit pool, which could squeeze a key revenue stream worth billions.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Automakers Drop Out of Carbon Credit Pool with Tesla: 04 March, 2026, 12:50 PM PST, CleanTechnica** Several automakers like Ford, Honda, and Toyota have pulled out of the EU carbon credit pooling arrangement with Tesla, where Tesla helped them meet fleet emission targets in exchange for payments. This shift matters because regulatory credits have been a major revenue source for Tesla, contributing billions and supporting its profitability during EV ramp-ups. For the industry, it signals legacy carmakers gaining confidence in their own EV fleets, potentially reducing Tesla's leverage in Europe. Source: https://cleantechnica.com/2026/03/04/automakers-drop-out-of-carbon-credit-pool-with-tesla/
2. **Tesla Takes Top Spot in Global Supply Chain Sustainability Rankings, Again: 04 March, 2026, 11:49 AM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Tesla topped the "Lead the Charge" leaderboard for supply chain sustainability and human rights, boosting its score by six points and outpacing Ford. This recognition highlights Tesla's efforts in ethical sourcing, which is crucial for its brand as consumers and regulators demand more transparency in EV production. It positions Tesla as a leader in the industry, potentially attracting talent and partnerships while pressuring competitors to step up. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/04/tesla-takes-top-spot-in-global-supply-chain-sustainability-rankings/
3. **Hight Logistics Highlights New Tesla Semi as It Expands Zero-Emission Fleet: 04 March, 2026, 11:38 AM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Hight Logistics showcased its new Tesla Semi during a delivery at a logistics conference, marking progress in its shift to electric trucking. This matters for Tesla's business as real-world fleet adoptions validate the Semi's viability, helping to build momentum in the heavy-duty EV market. For customers in logistics, it means lower emissions and operating costs, pushing the industry toward sustainable freight solutions. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/04/hight-logistics-highlights-new-tesla-semi-as-it-expands-zero-emission-fleet/
4. **Tesla Full Self-Driving v14.2.2.5 might be the most confusing release ever: 04 March, 2026, 11:24 AM PST, Teslarati** The latest FSD update shows extreme improvements in some areas but notable regressions in others, making it a mixed bag for users. This reflects the challenges of iterating on complex AI software, which is central to Tesla's tech edge but can frustrate customers expecting consistent progress. It underscores the need for Tesla to smooth out these variances to build trust in autonomous driving. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/tesla-full-self-driving-v14-2-2-5-might-be-most-confusing-release-ever/
5. **Tesla Hiring Senior Engineers in Berlin for ‘Robotaxi’ Autonomous Vehicle Team: 04 March, 2026, 11:18 AM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Tesla is recruiting senior electrical engineers in Berlin to work on circuit boards for its Robotaxi program. This hiring push signals acceleration in Tesla's autonomous vehicle development, key for expanding into ride-hailing services. For the industry, it highlights Tesla's global talent strategy, potentially speeding up Robotaxi deployment in Europe. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/04/tesla-hiring-senior-engineers-in-berlin-for-robotaxi-autonomous-vehicle-team/
6. **Tesla gets tip of the hat from major Wall Street firm on self-driving prowess: 04 March, 2026, 09:55 AM PST, Teslarati** Bank of America praised Tesla's camera-only autonomous approach as cheaper and more scalable than competitors' multi-sensor systems. This endorsement could boost investor confidence in Tesla's self-driving tech, a core part of its valuation. It means Tesla might gain a profitable edge i...

item.title: Ep 396: Tesla raised the Cybertruck AWD price by $10K to $69,990 after strong initial demand.
item.description:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** March 02, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $402.51 ▲ $0.71 (0.2%)
**HOOK:** Tesla raised the Cybertruck AWD price by $10K to $69,990 after strong initial demand.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Tesla’s Cybertruck Map Easter Egg Was Discovered by Owen Sparks** — 01 March, 2026, 11:08 AM PST, WhatsUpTesla Owen Sparks spotted a satellite map connecting Tesla's Austin HQ, the Neuralink facility in Del Valle, and the SpaceX/Boring Company site in Bastrop, forming a Cybertruck-shaped outline. This clever Easter egg highlights Tesla's growing Texas ecosystem and Elon's interconnected ventures, potentially boosting brand engagement among fans. It underscores how Tesla uses fun, hidden features to strengthen community ties and showcase its bold vision for integrated tech hubs. Source: https://whatsuptesla.com/2026/03/01/teslas-cybertruck-map-easter-egg-was-discovered-by-owen-sparks/
2. **Tesla Raises Cybertruck AWD Price As Promised, But by $10K** — 01 March, 2026, 7:14 AM PST, Drive Tesla Tesla increased the price of its new Dual Motor Cybertruck AWD from $59,990 to $69,990 just ten days after launch, following CEO Elon Musk's hint at adjustments based on demand. This move reflects strong customer interest but could test affordability in a competitive EV truck market, impacting sales volume. For Tesla's business, it signals confidence in the product's appeal while helping manage production scaling and profitability. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/tesla-raises-cybertruck-awd-price-as-promised-but-by-10k/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=tesla-raises-cybertruck-awd-price-as-promised-but-by-10k
3. **Tesla FSD Transfer Policy Change: What It Means and Who Still Qualifies** — 01 March, 2026, 6:11 AM PST, Drive Tesla Tesla updated its FSD (Supervised) transfer program eligibility, clarifying that many customers no longer qualify, which has disappointed those expecting to move the feature to new vehicles. This policy shift affects customer loyalty and perceived value of the $12,000 add-on, potentially slowing upgrades. It matters for Tesla's software revenue strategy, as it encourages fresh purchases but risks alienating long-time owners in a maturing autonomous tech landscape. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/tesla-fsd-transfer-policy-change-what-it-means-and-who-still-qualifies/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=tesla-fsd-transfer-policy-change-what-it-means-and-who-still-qualifies
4. **SpaceX Eyeing Massive $1.75 Trillion Valuation in Potential June IPO** — 01 March, 2026, 5:00 AM PST, TeslaNorth.com SpaceX is preparing for a possible IPO in June at a $1.75 trillion valuation, marking a shift from private status and highlighting its aerospace dominance. While not directly Tesla, this could indirectly benefit Tesla through shared leadership under Elon Musk, potentially freeing up resources or synergies in tech development. For the industry, it sets a benchmark for high-valuation tech IPOs amid growing interest in space ventures. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/01/spacex-eyeing-massive-1-75-trillion-valuation-in-potential-june-ipo/

1. **Texas Tech Hub Synergies Heating Up** - Community buzz around interconnected Elon Musk projects in Texas is growing. Fans are excited about how Tesla's Austin presence ties into broader innovations like boring tunnels and brain interfaces, sparking discussions on future urban mobility. This trend highlights Tesla's role in a larger ecosystem, making it stand out as more than just a car company. Source: https://whatsuptesla.com/2026/03/01/teslas-cybertruck-map-easter-egg-was-discovered-by-owen-sparks/

item.title: Ep 394: Tesla confirms upgraded black headliner and 16-inch screen for new Model Y orders in Canada, enhancing customer appeal.
item.description:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** February 28, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $402.51 ▼ $4.27 (1.0%)
**HOOK:** Tesla confirms upgraded black headliner and 16-inch screen for new Model Y orders in Canada, enhancing customer appeal.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Rivian Launches RAD: A New Performance Division to Push Its EVs Even Further: 27 February, 2026, 10:23 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Rivian has officially launched its Rivian Adventure Department (RAD), an in-house team focused on boosting the performance of its electric vehicles through innovative engineering. This move signals Rivian's push to compete more aggressively in the high-performance EV segment, potentially challenging Tesla's dominance in areas like off-road and adventure-focused models. For the industry, it highlights the growing emphasis on specialized divisions to accelerate EV innovation, which could pressure Tesla to continue evolving its Cybertruck and other rugged offerings. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/rivian-launches-rad-a-new-performance-division-to-push-its-evs-even-further/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rivian-launches-rad-a-new-performance-division-to-push-its-evs-even-further
2. **High Above the Clouds: How Long-Haul Flights Are Becoming Truly Connected for French Travelers: 27 February, 2026, 10:05 AM PST, WhatsUpTesla** Air France has rolled out free high-speed Wi-Fi on long-haul flights to destinations like Guadeloupe and Martinique, powered by SpaceX's Starlink technology. This development showcases how Starlink is transforming connectivity in aviation, making flights more productive for passengers and crediting Elon Musk's team for the advancement. For Tesla's broader ecosystem, it underscores the synergies between SpaceX and Tesla in advancing tech infrastructure, potentially boosting investor confidence in Musk-led ventures. Source: https://whatsuptesla.com/2026/02/27/high-above-the-clouds-how-long-haul-flights-are-becoming-truly-connected-for-french-travelers/
3. **Tesla confirms black headliner and 16” screen for new Canadian Model Y orders: 27 February, 2026, 7:13 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Tesla has updated its Canadian Model Y configurator to include a black headliner and a larger 16-inch rear screen, features that were previously unavailable in the region. This upgrade improves the interior experience for customers, aligning Canadian options with those in other markets and likely boosting sales amid competitive pressures. From a business perspective, it demonstrates Tesla's responsiveness to regional demands, helping maintain market share in Canada's growing EV sector. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/model-y/tesla-confirms-black-headliner-and-16-screen-for-new-canadian-model-y-orders/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=tesla-confirms-black-headliner-and-16-screen-for-new-canadian-model-y-orders
4. **Internal Records Show Government Knew iZEV Was Nearing Empty Before Tesla’s $43M Claims: 27 February, 2026, 6:24 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Internal documents reveal that Transport Canada knew the iZEV rebate program was running low on funds as early as November 2024, yet failed to notify the public before Tesla submitted $43 million in claims. This oversight could affect Tesla's reimbursement and highlights regulatory hurdles in EV incentives, potentially impacting sales strategies in Canada. For the industry, it raises questions about the reliability of government subsidies, pushing companies like Tesla to advocate for more stable support mechanisms. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/internal-records-show-government-knew-izev-was-nearing-empty-before-teslas-43m-claims/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=internal-records-show-government-knew-izev-was-nearing-empty-before-teslas-43m-claims

item.title: Ep 393: Tesla's new affordable Cybertruck variant sees delivery estimates pushed to April 2027 amid surging demand.
item.description:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** February 27, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $399.50 ▼ $7.28 (1.8%)
**HOOK:** Tesla's new affordable Cybertruck variant sees delivery estimates pushed to April 2027 amid surging demand.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Tesla Model Y 7-Seater Returns to Europe on Premium AWD: 27 February, 2026, 10:09 AM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Tesla has reintroduced the seven-seat option for the Model Y in Europe, limited to the Premium Long Range All-Wheel Drive model, allowing customers in countries like Germany and France to add third-row jump seats. This move addresses strong demand for family-oriented EVs in the region, potentially boosting Tesla's sales by catering to larger households and competing more directly with rivals offering versatile seating. For Tesla's business, it strengthens its position in Europe's growing EV market where practicality drives adoption. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/02/27/tesla-model-y-7-seater-returns-to-europe-on-premium-awd/
2. **Tesla Model Y Canada Update: 16-Inch Screen & Black Headliner Arrive: 27 February, 2026, 10:03 AM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Tesla updated its Canadian Model Y configurator to include a standard black headliner and a larger 16-inch QHD rear screen on Premium and Performance trims. These enhancements improve interior aesthetics and passenger experience, making the vehicle more appealing in a competitive market where cabin features influence buyer decisions. This could help Tesla maintain its lead in Canada by aligning with customer feedback on premium touches. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/02/27/tesla-model-y-canada-update-16-inch-screen-black-headliner-arrive/
3. **Tesla Lands Major Powerwall Distribution Deal in Australia: 27 February, 2026, 9:50 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Tesla secured a nationwide distribution agreement with Supply Partners Group for its Powerwall home battery system in Australia, building on its already strong popularity there. This partnership expands access to energy storage solutions, which matters for Tesla's energy business by accelerating adoption in a market prone to grid instability and high solar penetration. It positions Tesla to capture more of the residential energy market amid growing demand for reliable backups. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/tesla-lands-major-powerwall-distribution-deal-in-australia/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=tesla-lands-major-powerwall-distribution-deal-in-australia
4. **Tesla drops latest hint that new Cybertruck trim is selling like hotcakes: 27 February, 2026, 9:41 AM PST, Teslarati** Tesla's online design studio now shows April 2027 delivery for new All-Wheel-Drive Cybertruck orders, indicating high demand for the more affordable variant. This reflects strong consumer interest in Tesla's electric truck lineup, which could drive revenue growth but also highlights production scaling challenges. For the industry, it underscores the appeal of accessible EV trucks in shifting transportation paradigms. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/tesla-new-cybertruck-delivery-date-pushed-back-once-again/
5. **Virgin Media O2 Launches Europe’s First Starlink Direct-to-Cell Service in the UK: 27 February, 2026, 9:30 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Virgin Media O2 activated Europe's first commercial Starlink direct-to-cell service, branded O2 Satellite, providing satellite-powered mobile data to UK customers. While tied to SpaceX, this advances Tesla-adjacent tech in connectivity, potentially benefiting future integrations like in-vehicle systems. It matters for the industry by expanding remote access, which could influence EV ecosystems reliant on constant data links. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/spacex/virgin-media-o2-launches-europes-first-starlink-direct-to-cell-service-in-the-uk/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=virgin-media-o2-launches-europes-first-starlink-direct-to-cell-service-in-the-uk
6. **Tesla extends financing offers in China for ...

item.title: Ep 395: Tesla's FSD (Supervised) fleet hits 8.4 billion cumulative miles, closing in on the 10 billion needed for unsupervised autonomy.
item.description:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** March 01, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $402.51 ▼ $4.27 (1.0%)
**HOOK:** Tesla's FSD (Supervised) fleet hits 8.4 billion cumulative miles, closing in on the 10 billion needed for unsupervised autonomy.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Tesla FSD (Supervised) fleet passes 8.4 billion cumulative miles: 28 February, 2026, 11:57 PM PST, Teslarati** Tesla's Full Self-Driving (Supervised) system has now exceeded 8.4 billion miles driven, as updated on the company's safety page, highlighting the rapid accumulation of real-world data for neural network training. This milestone matters for Tesla's autonomy ambitions, as it approaches Elon Musk's estimated 10 billion mile threshold to handle rare edge cases effectively, potentially accelerating regulatory approval for unsupervised driving. For the industry, it underscores how data volume drives AI advancements in self-driving tech, giving Tesla a competitive edge over rivals with smaller datasets. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/tesla-fsd-supervised-fleet-passes-8-4-billion-cumulative-miles/
2. **Tesla expands global FSD (Supervised) testing with Abu Dhabi trials: 28 February, 2026, 9:49 PM PST, Teslarati** Tesla has launched its first formal testing program for Full Self-Driving (Supervised) in Abu Dhabi, marking a key step in global expansion of the technology. This development is significant for Tesla's business as it opens new markets in the Middle East, where regulatory frameworks are adapting to autonomous tech, potentially boosting adoption and sales. For customers, it means more diverse real-world data to refine FSD, improving reliability in varied driving conditions like desert environments. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/tesla-global-fsd-testing-abu-dhabi/
3. **Tesla Rebrands Autopilot Features in New 2026.2.9 Software Update: 28 February, 2026, 9:18 PM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Tesla's latest software update renames "Navigate on Autopilot" to "Navigate on Autosteer" and updates hardware references like the FSD Computer, signaling a shift in branding for driver-assistance features. This matters for the technology as it clarifies supervised autonomy to users and regulators, reducing confusion amid scrutiny over self-driving claims. For customers, the rebranding could improve user experience by aligning terminology with actual capabilities, while hinting at future unsupervised evolutions. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/02/28/tesla-rebrands-autopilot-features-in-new-2026-2-9-software-update/
4. **Tesla Shares New Battery Care Tips, Warns Against Relying on Superchargers Daily: 28 February, 2026, 1:32 PM PST, Drive Tesla** Tesla updated the Model Y owner's manual with battery maintenance advice, emphasizing home charging over daily Supercharger use to preserve long-term battery health. This is important for customers as it provides practical guidance to maximize vehicle longevity and performance, addressing common concerns about degradation. For Tesla's business, it reinforces the ecosystem of home charging solutions, encouraging Wall Connector sales amid growing EV adoption. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/tesla-shares-new-battery-care-tips-warns-against-relying-on-superchargers-daily/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=tesla-shares-new-battery-care-tips-warns-against-relying-on-superchargers-daily
5. **Tesla Vehicles Finally Come To Africa: 28 February, 2026, 7:30 AM PST, CleanTechnica** Tesla announced that Model 3 and Model Y will be available in Morocco starting mid-2026, marking the company's first entry into an African market. This expansion matters for Tesla's international growth, tapping into emerging EV demand in regions with solar potential and infrastructure needs. For the industry, it signals broader global access to electric vehicles, potentially accelerating clean energy transitions in developing markets. Source: https://cleantechnica.com/2026/02/28/tesla-vehicles-finally-come-...

item.title: Ep 397: Tesla raised the Cybertruck's entry price to $69,990 just 10 days after its $59,990 launch, signaling strong initial demand.
item.description:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** March 03, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $403.32 ▲ $0.37 (0.1%)
**HOOK:** Tesla raised the Cybertruck's entry price to $69,990 just 10 days after its $59,990 launch, signaling strong initial demand.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Did Tesla Just Pull in a Year’s Worth of Orders for the Cybertruck?: 02 March, 2026, 8:55 PM PST, CleanTechnica** Tesla's new $59,990 AWD Cybertruck version sparked a massive wave of orders last week, potentially equaling a full year's worth based on early estimates. This surge highlights how competitive pricing can accelerate adoption in the electric pickup market, though it falls short of the original $49,990 promise from seven years ago. For Tesla, it means bolstered production ramps and revenue, but also pressure to deliver on backlog amid supply chain realities. Source: https://cleantechnica.com/2026/03/02/did-tesla-just-pull-in-a-years-worth-of-orders-for-the-cybertruck/
2. **Tesla Germany Operating At 40 Percent Capacity — Handlesblatt: 02 March, 2026, 5:09 PM PST, CleanTechnica** Tesla's Gigafactory in Germany is reportedly running at only 40% capacity, with a upcoming union vote that could shape the company's European future. This underutilization points to challenges like labor disputes and market demand fluctuations, impacting Tesla's global manufacturing efficiency. It matters for the industry as it underscores the hurdles of scaling EV production in regulated markets, potentially affecting Tesla's competitiveness in Europe. Source: https://cleantechnica.com/2026/03/02/tesla-germany-operating-at-40-percent-capacity-handlesblatt/
3. **Tesla Kills the $60,000 Cybertruck: Entry Price Jumps by $10,000: 02 March, 2026, 12:49 PM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Just 10 days after introducing a $59,990 Dual Motor Cybertruck, Tesla bumped the price to $69,990, following Elon Musk's warning of an imminent increase. This move reflects rapid adjustments to demand and costs, but it could frustrate potential buyers expecting affordability. For Tesla's business, it strengthens margins on a high-profile product while testing customer loyalty in a competitive truck segment. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/02/tesla-kills-the-60000-cybertruck-entry-price-jumps-by-10000/
4. **Tesla back on top as Norway’s EV market surges to 98% share in February: 02 March, 2026, 5:37 AM PST, Teslarati** Tesla reclaimed the top spot in Norway with 1,210 registrations and a 16.6% share, as the country's EV market hit an astonishing 98% penetration. This dominance illustrates Tesla's strong brand in mature EV markets, driven by incentives and infrastructure. It signals broader industry trends toward electrification, benefiting Tesla's sales strategy but highlighting the need for continued innovation to maintain leads. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/tesla-norway-98-percent-ev-sales/

This case underscores security risks for EV makers amid growing vandalism concerns tied to environmental or competitive tensions. For Tesla, it emphasizes the importance of robust asset protection, potentially influencing insurance and operational costs in vulnerable areas. # Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** March 03, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $403.32 ▲ $0.37 (0.1%)
**HOOK:** Tesla raised the Cybertruck's entry price to $69,990 just 10 days after its $59,990 launch, signaling strong initial demand.

This surge highlights how competitive pricing can accelerate adoption in the electric pickup market, though it falls short of the original $49,990 promise from seven years ago. For Tesla, it means bolstered production ramps and revenue, but also pressure to deliver on backlog amid supply chain realities. Source: https://cleantechnica.com/2026/03/02/did-tesla-just-pull-in-a-years-worth-of-orders-for-the-cybertruck/

For Tesla's business, it strengthens margins on a high-profile product while testing customer loyalty in a competitive truck segment. item.title: Ep 399: Tesla's Giga Berlin workers elect 'Giga United' majority, signaling a shift away from union influence.
item.description:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** March 05, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $405.94 ▼ $0.80 (0.2%)
**HOOK:** Tesla's Giga Berlin workers elect 'Giga United' majority, signaling a shift away from union influence.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Starlink For General Aviation See Major Price Increase With 100 MPH Speed Cap: 04 March, 2026, 4:05 PM PST, Drive Tesla** Starlink has raised prices for its satellite internet service in small planes and added a 100 MPH speed cap, prompting backlash from general aviation pilots who used it for in-flight connectivity. This change could limit Starlink's appeal in the aviation sector, where reliable high-speed internet is crucial for navigation and communication, potentially affecting SpaceX's growth in non-traditional markets. While not directly Tesla, it highlights challenges in Elon's ecosystem of companies scaling innovative tech amid user expectations. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/spacex/starlink-for-general-aviation-see-major-price-increase-with-100-mph-speed-cap/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=starlink-for-general-aviation-see-major-price-increase-with-100-mph-speed-cap
2. **Tesla Giga Berlin Works Council Vote Backs ‘Giga United’ as IG Metall Support Falls: 04 March, 2026, 1:25 PM PST, Drive Tesla** In the latest works council election at Tesla's Giga Berlin, the independent 'Giga United' group secured a majority, while support for the IG Metall union declined significantly. This outcome reflects employee preferences for internal representation over traditional unions, which could streamline operations and reduce labor disputes at Tesla's key European factory. It matters for Tesla's business as it strengthens company-aligned governance, potentially boosting productivity in a competitive EV market. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/tesla-giga-berlin-works-council-vote-backs-giga-united-as-ig-metall-support-falls/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=tesla-giga-berlin-works-council-vote-backs-giga-united-as-ig-metall-support-falls
3. **Tesla Opens Select Superchargers in Malaysia to Other EVs: 04 March, 2026, 9:59 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Tesla has opened four Supercharger stations in Malaysia to non-Tesla electric vehicles, expanding access to its fast-charging network for the first time in the country. This move supports broader EV adoption in Southeast Asia by making infrastructure more inclusive, which could increase utilization rates and revenue for Tesla while pressuring competitors to improve their own networks. For customers, it means more convenient charging options, fostering a healthier industry ecosystem. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/tesla-opens-select-superchargers-in-malaysia-to-other-evs/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=tesla-opens-select-superchargers-in-malaysia-to-other-evs
4. **Tesla Model Y Drives Australian Sales Rebound in February: 04 March, 2026, 9:27 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Tesla's Model Y led a sales recovery in Australia last month, topping EV charts after a slow January start, according to early Electric Vehicle Council data. This rebound underscores the Model Y's popularity and Tesla's resilience in fluctuating markets, helping to maintain momentum in a key international region amid global demand challenges. It signifies positive implications for Tesla's business, as strong sales in markets like Australia contribute to overall volume and brand strength. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/tesla-model-y-drives-australian-sales-rebound-in-february/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=tesla-model-y-drives-australian-sales-rebound-in-february
5. **Tesla owners in South Korea log over 8 million km using FSD (Supervised) in just 100 days: 04 March, 2026, 7:46 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Tesla owners in South Korea have driven over 8 million kilometers using Full Self-Driving (Supervised) since its rollout, showcasing rapid adoption in a tech-savvy market. This mil...

item.title: Ep 401: Tesla patents breakthrough dry electrode process for cheaper, cleaner battery production.
item.description:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** March 08, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $396.73 ▼ $8.24 (2.0%)
**HOOK:** Tesla patents breakthrough dry electrode process for cheaper, cleaner battery production.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Tesla Robotaxi Prices Jump in Austin: Here is the New Cost for a 5-Mile Trip: 07 March, 2026, 2:22 PM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Tesla raised its Robotaxi base fare in Austin from $1 to $3.25, pushing a typical 5-mile trip from $6 to $8.25 while keeping the per-mile rate at $1. This adjustment could help Tesla boost revenue from shorter rides as Robotaxi expands, but it might deter price-sensitive customers in a competitive ride-hailing market. For users, it means reassessing costs for daily commutes, potentially affecting adoption rates in key test cities. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/07/tesla-robotaxi-prices-jump-in-austin-here-is-the-new-cost-for-a-5-mile-trip/
2. **Gail’s TESLA Podcast Ep. 162: Robotaxi We Love You! 8-Segment Rides in Texas + mini Cybertruck spot in SF: 07 March, 2026, 1:01 PM PST, WhatsUpTesla.com** The latest podcast episode celebrates unsupervised Robotaxi rides in Texas with footage from mid-2025 onward, plus a sighting of a mini Cybertruck in San Francisco. It highlights how Tesla's autonomy is becoming smoother and more reliable, which matters for building public trust in self-driving tech. For the industry, this underscores Tesla's lead in real-world deployment, potentially pressuring competitors to accelerate their own programs. Source: https://whatsuptesla.com/2026/03/07/gails-tesla-podcast-ep-162-robotaxi-we-love-you-8-segment-rides-in-texas-mini-cybertruck-spot-in-sf/
3. **Why Starlink Users are Furious Over the Sudden New “In-Motion” Speed Caps: 07 March, 2026, 9:32 AM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Starlink disabled in-motion data for its $5 monthly standby plan, limiting Mini dish users to under 10 mph for internet access without warning. This change frustrates mobile users like RVers and impacts Tesla owners who integrate Starlink for on-road connectivity. It highlights challenges in balancing service tiers with network demands, potentially affecting Tesla's ecosystem partnerships. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/07/why-starlink-users-are-furious-over-the-sudden-new-in-motion-speed-caps/
4. **Tesla Cracks the ‘Holy Grail’ of Battery Making with New Patent: 07 March, 2026, 9:24 AM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Tesla's new patent introduces a dry electrode process that eliminates toxic solvents and cuts manufacturing costs, confirmed by Elon Musk as a production breakthrough. This could revolutionize battery efficiency for EVs and energy storage, reducing environmental impact and supply chain dependencies. For Tesla's business, it positions the company to scale production faster and compete more aggressively on price. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/07/tesla-cracks-the-holy-grail-of-battery-making-with-new-patent/
5. **Tesla Sales in Germany Actually Still Down Enormously: 07 March, 2026, 7:57 AM PST, CleanTechnica** Despite recent buzz about rising sales, Tesla's deliveries in Germany remain significantly lower than previous peaks, continuing a downward trend amid broader European challenges. This reflects ongoing market headwinds like subsidies ending and competition from local brands, which could force Tesla to adapt its pricing and marketing strategies. For the industry, it signals that EV adoption isn't uniform globally, affecting long-term growth forecasts. Source: https://cleantechnica.com/2026/03/07/tesla-sales-in-germany-actually-still-down-enormously/
6. **Tesla preps to build its most massive Supercharger yet: 400+ V4 stalls: 07 March, 2026, 3:37 AM PST, Teslarati** Tesla is expanding the Eddie World Supercharger in Yermo, California, to over 400 V4 stalls in phases, creating the largest site yet. This move supports growing EV demand by addressing charging bottlenecks, crucial for long-haul travel and fl...

item.title: Ep 403: Tesla loses two long-time executives, including finance VP Sendil Palani after 17 years and OTA director Thomas Dmytryk after 11 years.
item.description:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** March 10, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $398.68 ▲ $1.84 (0.5%)
**HOOK:** Tesla loses two long-time executives, including finance VP Sendil Palani after 17 years and OTA director Thomas Dmytryk after 11 years.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Tesla Sweden’s Megapack Supercharger near Arlanda continues to aggravate IF Metall union: 10 March, 2026, 2:51 AM PST, Teslarati** Tesla's charging site in Arlandastad is still running despite the union's blockade amid ongoing labor disputes in Sweden. This highlights Tesla's resilience in pushing forward with infrastructure projects even in contentious environments, potentially strengthening its European energy and charging network while underscoring broader tensions between innovative tech firms and traditional labor unions. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/tesla-sweden-megapack-supercharger-aggravates-if-metall-union/
2. **Tesla Finance Veteran Sendil Palani Departs After 17-Year Run: 09 March, 2026, 3:13 PM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Sendil Palani, Tesla's VP of Finance since 2009, has left the company after helping guide it from startup to global leader. His departure could signal a shift in Tesla's financial leadership at a time when the company is scaling robotaxi and energy operations, though it also opens opportunities for fresh perspectives in managing Tesla's ambitious growth trajectory. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/09/tesla-finance-veteran-sendil-palani-departs-after-17-year-run/
3. **Tesla loses Director who designed one of the company’s best features: 09 March, 2026, 1:54 PM PST, Teslarati** Thomas Dmytryk, key developer of Tesla's Over-the-Air updates, has departed after over 11 years, leaving a gap in software innovation expertise. This loss matters for Tesla's product evolution, as OTA updates have been crucial for customer satisfaction and vehicle improvements, potentially affecting how the company maintains its edge in software-driven automotive tech. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/tesla-loses-director-who-designed-one-of-the-companys-best-features/
4. **Tesla’s most wanted Model Y heads to new region with no sign of U.S. entry: 09 March, 2026, 12:28 PM PST, Teslarati** Tesla is expanding its longer-wheelbase Model Y L variant to a new market, offering better seating and space for families without third-row compromises. This move bolsters Tesla's presence in regions prioritizing practical EVs, but the lack of U.S. plans highlights regulatory or market differences that could limit options for American customers seeking versatile electric SUVs. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/tesla-most-wanted-model-y-heads-to-new-region-with-no-sign-of-u-s-entry/
5. **Tesla Spotted Shipping 30+ Cybercabs With Steering Wheels Ahead of April Launch: 09 March, 2026, 10:45 AM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Over 30 Cybercabs were seen being transported from Giga Texas, some equipped with steering wheels, as Tesla prepares for production starting in April. This indicates ramp-up in testing and manufacturing for autonomous vehicles, which could transform urban mobility and boost Tesla's revenue through robotaxi fleets if regulatory hurdles are cleared. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/09/tesla-spotted-shipping-30-cybercabs-with-steering-wheels-ahead-of-april-launch/
6. **Tesla Now Operates More than 2,500 Superchargers and 12,000 Supercharger Stalls Across China: 09 March, 2026, 9:11 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Tesla has surpassed 2,500 Supercharger stations in mainland China, enhancing accessibility for EV owners in one of the world's largest markets. This expansion supports Tesla's growth strategy in Asia, improving customer experience and potentially increasing vehicle adoption by addressing range anxiety in a competitive landscape. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/tesla-now-operates-more-than-2500-superchargers-and-12000-supercharger-stalls-across-china/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=tesla-now-opera...

6. **Tesla Now Operates More than 2,500 Superchargers and 12,000 Supercharger Stalls Across China: 09 March, 2026, 9:11 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Tesla has surpassed 2,500 Supercharger stations in mainland China, enhancing accessibility for EV owners in one of the world's largest markets. This expansion supports Tesla's growth strategy in Asia, improving customer experience and potentially increasing vehicle adoption by addressing range anxiety in a competitive landscape. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/tesla-now-operates-more-than-2500-superchargers-and-12000-supercharger-stalls-across-china/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=tesla-now-opera...
item.itunes:title: Ep 403: Tesla loses two long-time executives, including finance VP Sendil Palani after 17 years and OTA director Thomas Dmytryk after 11 years.

item.title: Ep 405: Tesla is ramping up Cybercab production to hundreds weekly at its Austin factory ahead of launch.
item.description:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** March 12, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $407.82 ▲ $3.01 (0.7%)
**HOOK:** Tesla is ramping up Cybercab production to hundreds weekly at its Austin factory ahead of launch.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Tesla Cybercab production line is targeting hundreds of vehicles weekly: report (Teslarati)** — 12 March, 2026, 3:09 AM PST, Teslarati Tesla is hiring more staff and installing new equipment at Giga Texas to produce hundreds of Cybercabs per week, signaling a major push toward robotaxi deployment. This ramp-up matters for Tesla's autonomous vehicle ambitions, potentially accelerating revenue from ride-hailing services while addressing production bottlenecks in the EV industry. For customers, it means faster access to affordable, self-driving transport options. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/tesla-cybercab-line-targeting-hundreds-weekly-report/
2. **Elon Musk Reveals Tesla-xAI Project “Macrohard” Aimed at Disrupting Software Companies (Drive Tesla)** — 11 March, 2026, 3:21 PM PST, Drive Tesla Elon Musk announced Macrohard, a joint Tesla-xAI project to revolutionize software through AI that automates complex tasks. This collaboration highlights Tesla's expansion into AI beyond vehicles, potentially disrupting traditional software firms and enhancing Tesla's tech ecosystem. It means stronger integration of AI in Tesla products, benefiting the industry by pushing boundaries in automation efficiency. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/elon-musk-reveals-tesla-xai-project-macrohard-aimed-at-disrupting-software-companies/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=elon-musk-reveals-tesla-xai-project-macrohard-aimed-at-disrupting-software-companies
3. **Tesla Cybercab Appears at USDOT in Washington as Volume Launch Nears (TeslaNorth.com)** — 11 March, 2026, 3:07 PM PST, TeslaNorth.com Tesla showcased its production Cybercab at the U.S. Department of Transportation during a federal autonomous vehicle safety forum. This regulatory engagement is crucial for gaining approvals needed for widespread robotaxi operations, impacting Tesla's business by smoothing the path to market entry. For the industry, it sets precedents in safety standards for autonomous tech. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/11/tesla-cybercab-appears-at-usdot-in-washington-as-volume-launch-nears/
4. **Tesla VP explains latest updates in trade secret theft case (Teslarati)** — 11 March, 2026, 1:07 PM PST, Teslarati A Tesla VP detailed how former supplier Matthews allegedly stole and sold proprietary tech to competitors, leading to a $1 billion lawsuit. This case underscores risks in Tesla's supply chain, affecting business by emphasizing the need for stronger IP protection in the competitive EV sector. It highlights broader industry challenges with technology theft amid rapid innovation. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/tesla-vp-explains-latest-updates-trade-secret-theft-case/
5. **Tesla Posts Strong February in China with 58,599 Vehicles Sold (Drive Tesla)** — 11 March, 2026, 10:06 AM PST, Drive Tesla Tesla sold nearly double the vehicles in China last February compared to the prior year, driven by Gigafactory Shanghai's output. This growth bolsters Tesla's position in the world's largest EV market, supporting business resilience against global slowdowns. For customers, it means better availability and potential price stability in a key region. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/tesla-posts-strong-february-in-china-with-58599-vehicles-sold/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=tesla-posts-strong-february-in-china-with-58599-vehicles-sold
6. **Tesla Imports Surge in South Korea as EV Demand Grows (Drive Tesla)** — 11 March, 2026, 9:04 AM PST, Drive Tesla Tesla's vehicle registrations in South Korea have skyrocketed early in 2026, fueled by rising EV adoption. This surge strengthens Tesla's international sales footprint, contributing to revenue diversification be...

item.title: Ep 407: Tesla's latest software update brings Comfort Braking to Model Y, enhancing smoother stops for owners.
item.description:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** March 14, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $391.20 ▼ $4.28 (1.1%)
**HOOK:** Tesla's latest software update brings Comfort Braking to Model Y, enhancing smoother stops for owners.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Tesla Update 2026.8 Adds Comfort Braking, Spotify Upgrade and New Cybertruck Safety Feature: 13 March, 2026, 03:27 PM PST, Drive Tesla** Tesla rolled out software update 2026.8, introducing Comfort Braking for gentler stops on the Model Y, an upgraded Spotify interface across vehicles, and a new safety alert for Cybertruck owners. This matters because it shows Tesla's commitment to refining the driving experience through over-the-air updates, potentially boosting owner satisfaction and loyalty in a competitive EV market. For customers, these tweaks mean more comfortable rides and better integration of entertainment, keeping Tesla ahead in user-focused tech. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/tesla-update-2026-8-adds-comfort-braking-spotify-upgrade-and-new-cybertruck-safety-feature/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=tesla-update-2026-8-adds-comfort-braking-spotify-upgrade-and-new-cybertruck-safety-feature
2. **Tesla Robotaxi App Hints at Android Launch and Enhanced Privacy: 13 March, 2026, 03:06 PM PST, TeslaNorth.com** A decompile of Tesla's Robotaxi app version 26.2.0 reveals ongoing work on Android compatibility to match iOS features, plus stronger data security measures for users. This is significant for Tesla's business as it expands the Robotaxi network's accessibility, potentially increasing adoption among Android users and strengthening its position in autonomous ride-hailing. For the industry, it highlights Tesla's focus on privacy amid growing concerns, which could set a standard for secure mobility services. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/03/13/tesla-robotaxi-app-hints-at-android-launch-and-enhanced-privacy/
3. **Jowua active cooling phone mount review: fast charging without the overheating [Deal]: 13 March, 2026, 12:05 PM PST, Drive Tesla** The Jowua active cooling phone mount for Tesla vehicles was reviewed, praising its ability to fast-charge phones without overheating during navigation or calls. This accessory addresses a common pain point for Tesla owners who rely on their phones in-car, improving usability and safety on long drives. It underscores how third-party innovations are complementing Tesla's ecosystem, giving customers more options to customize their experience without waiting for official updates. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/jowua-active-cooling-phone-mount-review-fast-charging-without-the-overheating-deal/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=jowua-active-cooling-phone-mount-review-fast-charging-without-the-overheating-deal
4. **Elon Musk: Surprise Remote Talk at 2026 Abundance Summit – My Full Verbatim Transcript: 13 March, 2026, 09:00 AM PST, WhatsUpTesla** Elon Musk made a surprise appearance at the 2026 Abundance Summit, discussing Grok 4.20, AI advancements, Optimus robot timelines, and paths to post-scarcity abundance. This talk matters for Tesla's technology trajectory, as it outlines ambitious timelines for AI and robotics that could transform industries beyond automotive. For investors and enthusiasts, it provides insight into Tesla's long-term vision, potentially influencing perceptions of its innovation edge. Source: https://whatsuptesla.com/2026/03/13/elon-musk-surprise-remote-talk-at-2026-abundance-summit-my-full-verbatim-transcript/
5. **Honda Cancels Three Major EVs in North America as Losses Mount: 13 March, 2026, 08:01 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Honda announced the cancellation of three planned electric vehicles in North America due to significant financial losses and declining public interest in EVs. This development highlights challenges in the broader EV industry, where traditional automakers are struggling to compete, potentially creating opportunities for Tes...
